description: Receives a CSV file via webhook, cleans the data by standardizing phone numbers, trimming whitespace, and removing rows with missing name or email, then returns the cleaned data as JSON.

trigger.description: Accepts a raw CSV body (Content-Type: text/plain or text/csv) and returns cleaned JSON data. Requires Header Auth (X-API-Key) for access. Multipart/form-data is not supported — send the raw CSV as the request body. If the workflow encounters an uncaught runtime error before reaching a Respond to Webhook node, the caller will receive a timeout response (30s); a reverse proxy should enforce a shorter upstream timeout and return 504 in that case.

steps.name: Receive CSV Webhook
steps.description: Listens for incoming POST requests containing CSV data in the request body. Protected by Header Auth using the csv-cleaner-api-key credential. Only raw body (text/plain or text/csv) is supported; multipart/form-data uploads are not supported.
steps.error_handling: Return 401 if auth header missing or invalid; return 400 if no body received

steps.name: Normalize Raw Body
steps.description: Extracts and normalizes the raw CSV body from the webhook output so the Code node has a consistent raw_body field. Only handles string and simple object bodies — binary handling is deferred to the Code node. Note: multipart/form-data is not supported; only raw body requests are expected.

output_shape.raw_body: string containing the CSV text, or empty string if body was not a plain string

steps.error_handling: Defaults to empty string if body is absent or not a string; binary fallback handled in step_3

steps.name: Parse and Clean CSV Data
steps.description: Parses the raw CSV string (handling both LF and CRLF line endings, and RFC 4180 escaped quotes), normalizes all header keys to lowercase at parse time, trims whitespace from all fields, standardizes phone numbers to +1-XXX-XXX-XXXX format, and removes rows missing name or email. Returns success flag and error details for downstream routing. Invalid phone rows are collected after the full row is assembled to ensure complete row data. Error messages are static strings that never echo back input data to avoid PII leakage.

output_shape.error: null on success, static descriptive string on failure — never echoes input data
output_shape.cleaned_rows: array of cleaned row objects with lowercase keys
output_shape.invalid_phone_rows: array of fully-assembled cleaned row objects with non-standard phone numbers, each with original_phone preserved

steps.error_handling: Returns success:false with static error string for empty body, missing CSV rows, or parse failures rather than throwing. Invalid phone numbers are flagged after full row assembly. Error messages never contain input data to prevent PII leakage.

steps.name: Validate Cleaned Data Exists
steps.description: Routes to success branch if the Code node reported success:true, otherwise routes to error branch.

output_shape.false_branch: success:false — empty body, no rows, or parse error

steps.error_handling: Routes to error response on false branch

steps.name: Build Success Response
steps.description: Constructs the final JSON response with status, cleaned rows, invalid phone row flags, and summary stats.

output_shape.invalid_phone_rows: array of rows with non-standard phone numbers

steps.error_handling: Surface any expression errors in workflow log

steps.name: Build Error Response
steps.description: Returns a structured error JSON response when data cleaning or validation fails. Error message is sourced from the Code node's static error field — never echoes back input data to prevent PII leakage in logs or intermediate proxies.

assignments.name: message
assignments.value: ={{ $json.error || 'Failed to parse or clean CSV data. Ensure the request body contains valid CSV with at least a header row.' }}

output_shape.message: static string describing the failure — never contains input data

steps.error_handling: Always succeeds — this is itself the error handler. Maintain invariant: error messages must remain static and must not include any input field values.

steps.name: Respond Success to Webhook
steps.description: Sends a controlled HTTP 200 response back to the caller with the cleaned data JSON object. responseBody is set to the raw JSON object so n8n serializes it correctly — avoids double-encoded JSON that would result from JSON.stringify.

output_shape.body: JSON object with status, data, invalid_phone_rows, and summary

steps.error_handling: If expression fails, n8n will surface an error in the workflow log. Note: if this node is never reached due to an uncaught upstream error, the webhook caller will hang until the 30s global timeout; a reverse proxy should enforce a shorter upstream timeout.

steps.name: Respond Error to Webhook
steps.description: Sends a controlled HTTP 400 response back to the caller with the error details JSON object. responseBody is set to the raw JSON object so n8n serializes it correctly — avoids double-encoded JSON.

output_shape.body: JSON object with status=error and descriptive static message

steps.error_handling: Always succeeds — terminates the error branch with a proper HTTP response

error_handling.on_workflow_failure: If an uncaught runtime error occurs before a Respond to Webhook node is reached (e.g. in step_2 or step_3), the caller will hang until the 30s global timeout because n8n does not automatically send an HTTP response in responseNode mode. Configure a reverse proxy (e.g. nginx) upstream of n8n to enforce a shorter connection timeout and return 504 to callers. Optionally configure an n8n Error Workflow to send a 500 JSON response via a Respond to Webhook node.
error_handling.pii_note: Error messages throughout the workflow are static strings and must never be modified to include input field values, to prevent PII leakage via error responses or intermediate proxy logs.

output.description: Returns HTTP 200 with JSON body containing status=success, cleaned data array (with lowercase keys), invalid_phone_rows array, and row count summary; or HTTP 400 with status=error and a static descriptive message. Both branches use explicit Respond to Webhook nodes with responseBody set to the raw JSON object to prevent double-encoding.

credentials_needed.name: csv-cleaner-api-key
credentials_needed.description: Header Auth credential used by the Webhook node. Callers must include the configured header (e.g., X-API-Key: <secret>) in every request. Configure via n8n Credentials manager.

security.pii_handling: CSV may contain PII (names, emails, phone numbers). Data is processed in-memory and not persisted. No external API calls made. Error messages are static strings and never echo back input data.
security.data_flow: Webhook (authenticated) → in-memory normalize → in-memory parse/clean → JSON response. No data stored or forwarded externally.
security.content_type_note: Only raw body requests (text/plain or text/csv) are supported. Multipart/form-data is not supported.

cost_estimate: $0.00 per run — no external API calls or paid services used

test_cases.name: Happy path — well-formed CSV with phone numbers

input.body:
name,email,phone
Alice Smith,alice@example.com,5551234567
 Bob Jones ,bob@example.com, (555) 987-6543 
Carol White,carol@example.com,+1 555 111 2222

test_cases.name: Edge case — rows missing name or email are removed

input.body:
name,email,phone
Alice Smith,alice@example.com,5551234567
,bob@example.com,5559876543
Carol White,,5551112222
Dave Brown,dave@example.com,5554445555

test_cases.name: Error case — empty or missing body

expected.message: Request body is empty or missing. Please provide a valid CSV payload.

test_cases.name: Edge case — CSV with extra whitespace and mixed-case phone column (keys normalized to lowercase)

input.body:
Name  ,  Email  ,  Phone  
  Jane Doe  ,  jane@example.com  ,  1-800-555-0199  
  John Roe  ,  john@example.com  ,  +15551230000

expected.note: Header keys are normalized to lowercase at parse time; output keys will be lowercase regardless of input casing.

test_cases.name: Error case — CSV with header row only and no data rows

expected.message: CSV contained no data rows. Ensure the file has a header row and at least one data row.

test_cases.name: Edge case — Windows CRLF line endings
Edge case — non-standard phone number flagged in invalid_phone_rows with full row data

expected.note: invalid_phone_rows entries contain the fully assembled cleaned row (all fields populated), not a partial row.

expected.description: n8n Webhook node rejects the request before workflow executes when Header Auth credential is not present or does not match.

test_cases.name: Edge case — RFC 4180 escaped quotes inside quoted fields
Edge case — ALL CAPS header keys normalized to lowercase

expected.note: ALL CAPS header keys (NAME, EMAIL, PHONE) are normalized to lowercase at parse time. Rows are correctly filtered and phone correctly detected.
